name: Salvador Allende
role: President of Chile (1970–1973) — Socialist; founder of Popular Unity coalition
quote: They may overcome us, but they cannot defeat the great avenues through which free people will walk to build a better society.

name: Augusto Pinochet
role: Commander-in-Chief of Army; Leader of Military Junta (1973–1990); President of Chile (1974–1990)
quote: I'm not a dictator. It's just that I have a grumpy face.

name: Henry Kissinger
role: US National Security Advisor (1969–1975); Secretary of State (1973–1977); directed US covert intervention in Chile
quote: I don't see why we need to stand by and watch a country go communist due to the irresponsibility of its own people.

name: Richard Nixon
role: US President (1969–1974); authorized CIA covert operations to prevent Allende's election and destabilize his government
quote: [Instructed CIA Director Helms]: Make the economy scream. I want to do anything we can to hurt Allende and anyone who is going to bring Communism to Chile.

name: Manuel Contreras
role: Director of DINA (1974–1977); chief architect of Operation Condor; convicted of Letelier assassination and multiple human rights crimes
quote: DINA was the arm of General Pinochet and everything it did was with his full knowledge and authorization.

name: Orlando Letelier
role: Allende's Foreign Minister and Defense Minister; exiled opposition leader; assassinated by DINA in Washington DC, September 1976
quote: The economic system imposed by the Chilean junta is inseparable from its political system of terror.

name: Víctor Jara
role: Folk musician, theater director, and Popular Unity activist; tortured and executed in National Stadium, September 16, 1973
quote: How hard it is to sing when I must sing of horror. Horror which I am living, horror which I am dying.

name: Cardinal Raúl Silva Henríquez
role: Archbishop of Santiago; founded Vicaría de la Solidaridad; key protector of human rights victims under the dictatorship
quote: The Church cannot remain silent when human beings are suffering, when the dignity of the person is threatened.

name: Patricio Aylwin
role: PDC president who initially opposed Allende; later led 'No' campaign and Concertación; President of Chile (1990–1994)
quote: Justice to the extent possible, given the need for reconciliation in Chilean society.

name: General Carlos Prats
role: Commander-in-Chief of Chilean Army; constitutionalist who supported Allende's democratic legitimacy; assassinated by DINA in Buenos Aires, 1974
quote: A military man who conspires against the constitutional government commits treason and dishonors the uniform.

name: Jaime Guzmán
role: Chief ideologist of the Pinochet regime; principal drafter of the 1980 constitution; founder of UDI party; assassinated by FPMR in 1991
quote: The constitution should be such that whoever wins the election can do very little of what they want.

name: General René Schneider
role: Commander-in-Chief of Chilean Army; constitutionalist shot in CIA-backed kidnapping attempt, October 1970; died October 25, 1970
quote: The Army is not a solution to political problems. The election of President Allende must be respected.

name: Michael Townley
role: US-born DINA agent; planted Letelier bomb; masterminded Prats assassination; became US government witness and entered witness protection
quote: [Congressional testimony]: I acted under orders from DINA. Everything I did was authorized by General Contreras and ultimately by Pinochet.

name: General Gustavo Leigh
role: Commander of Chilean Air Force; junta member; ordered the bombing of La Moneda; expelled from junta by Pinochet in 1978 for criticizing the regime
quote: We were ready to destroy La Moneda completely if necessary. We would have turned it to rubble.

name: Bernardo Leighton
role: Christian Democratic Party leader and former Chilean Vice President; shot by DINA-linked Italian neo-fascists in Rome, 1975; survived permanently disabled
quote: Chile will return to democracy. No dictatorship in history has been permanent. The people's will always prevails.

name: Bishop Sergio Valech
role: President of the 2003-2004 National Commission on Political Imprisonment and Torture (Valech Commission); documented 40,000+ torture survivors
quote: The testimonies I heard were not stories from other countries or another era. This happened here, in Chile, and it must never be forgotten.

name: Raúl Rettig
role: Former Senator; President of Chile's National Commission on Truth and Reconciliation (1990-1991); gave his name to the Rettig Report documenting 2,279 victims
quote: We cannot build a future on silence. The truth, however painful, is the foundation of reconciliation.

name: Jorge Cauas
role: Finance Minister under Pinochet (1974-1977); implemented the 'Chicago Boys' shock therapy economic reforms in April 1975
quote: The pain of adjustment is temporary; the benefits of a stable, free economy are permanent.

name: Joan Jara
role: British wife of Víctor Jara; human rights activist; founded Víctor Jara Foundation; successfully sued perpetrator Pedro Barrientos in US courts
quote: Víctor's voice could not be silenced by bullets. It continues to resonate wherever injustice exists in the world.

name: Paul Schäfer
role: German Nazi fugitive; founded Colonia Dignidad; collaborated with DINA providing remote torture and execution site; convicted of child abuse (2006)
quote: [Never publicly admitted crimes; claimed colony was a peaceful religious community]
